+/**
+ * Email notification adapter.
+ *
+ * Implements the NotificationAdapter interface for email delivery.
+ * Provides template rendering, recipient resolution, and structured
+ * email composition with both HTML and plain text output.
+ *
+ * ## Features
+ *
+ * - **Template rendering**: Generates HTML emails from named templates
+ *   with dynamic content. Select a template via `metadata.template`
+ *   in the NotificationPayload, or let the adapter auto-detect based
+ *   on the subject line.
+ * - **Recipient resolution**: Validates email addresses and supports
+ *   `"Display Name <email>"` format, extracting address and name.
+ * - **Priority-aware delivery**: Renders a priority banner in the
+ *   HTML output for `high` and `urgent` notifications.
+ *
+ * ## Usage
+ *
+ * ```typescript
+ * const emailAdapter = new EmailAdapter();
+ * notificationService.registerAdapter(emailAdapter);
+ * ```
+ *
+ * ## Templates
+ *
+ * Built-in templates:
+ * - `order-confirmation` — Order confirmation with item summary
+ * - `order-status-update` — Order status change notification
+ * - `welcome` — New user welcome email
+ * - `default` — Generic notification wrapper
+ *
+ * Select a template explicitly via `metadata.template`:
+ * ```typescript
+ * await service.dispatchNotification({
+ *   type: 'email',
+ *   recipient: 'alice@example.com',
+ *   subject: 'Welcome!',
+ *   body: 'Welcome to TaskFlow Pro.',
+ *   metadata: { template: 'welcome', recipientName: 'Alice' },
+ * });
+ * ```
+ *
+ * ## Replacing with a real provider
+ *
+ * To use a real email provider (SendGrid, SES, Mailgun), modify the
+ * `send()` method to call the provider API after template rendering.
+ * Wrap external API errors and return `{ success: false, error: '...' }`.
+ */
+import type { NotificationAdapter } from './notification.adapter.js';
+import type { NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationType, NotificationPriority } from '../models/notification.js';
+/**
+ * Supported email template names.
+ * Templates determine the HTML structure and styling of the email.
+ */
+export type EmailTemplateName = 'order-confirmation' | 'order-status-update' | 'welcome' | 'default';
+/**
+ * Rendered email content with both HTML and plain text versions.
+ */
+export interface RenderedEmail {
+    /** Full HTML email content with inline styles. */
+    html: string;
+    /** Plain text fallback for clients that don't support HTML. */
+    text: string;
+}
+/**
+ * Resolved recipient with optional display name.
+ */
+export interface ResolvedRecipient {
+    /** The validated email address. */
+    email: string;
+    /** Display name parsed from `"Name <email>"` format. */
+    name?: string;
+}
+/**
+ * An email message as stored by the adapter.
+ * Includes both the original content and the rendered output.
+ * Useful for testing and asserting on sent emails.
+ */
+export interface SentEmail {
+    /** Unique message identifier (e.g., `email-0001`). */
+    messageId: string;
+    /** Recipient email address. */
+    to: string;
+    /** Recipient display name, if resolved from the recipient string. */
+    toName?: string;
+    /** Email subject line. */
+    subject: string;
+    /** Original plain text body content. */
+    body: string;
+    /** Rendered HTML email content. */
+    html: string;
+    /** Notification priority level. */
+    priority: NotificationPriority;
+    /** Template used for rendering. */
+    template: EmailTemplateName;
+    /** When the email was "sent" (recorded). */
+    sentAt: Date;
+}
+/**
+ * Reset the message ID counter. For testing only.
+ */
+export declare function resetEmailMessageIdCounter(): void;
+export declare class EmailAdapter implements NotificationAdapter {
+    readonly type: NotificationType;
+    private logger;
+    private sentEmails;
+    /**
+     * Resolve and validate an email recipient string.
+     *
+     * Supports two formats:
+     * - Plain email: `"alice@example.com"`
+     * - Named email: `"Alice Smith <alice@example.com>"`
+     *
+     * @param recipient - The recipient string to resolve.
+     * @returns The resolved recipient, or `null` if the format is invalid.
+     */
+    resolveRecipient(recipient: string): ResolvedRecipient | null;
+    /**
+     * Select the appropriate email template for a notification payload.
+     *
+     * Template selection priority:
+     * 1. Explicit `metadata.template` value (if it's a valid template name)
+     * 2. Auto-detection from subject line patterns
+     * 3. Falls back to `'default'`
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification payload.
+     * @returns The template name to use for rendering.
+     */
+    selectTemplate(payload: NotificationPayload): EmailTemplateName;
+    /**
+     * Render an email template with the given data.
+     *
+     * Produces both HTML and plain text versions. The HTML output uses
+     * inline styles for maximum email client compatibility.
+     *
+     * @param template - The template name to render.
+     * @param data - Content and metadata for the template.
+     * @returns The rendered email with HTML and plain text content.
+     */
+    renderTemplate(template: EmailTemplateName, data: {
+        subject: string;
+        body: string;
+        recipientName?: string;
+        priority: NotificationPriority;
+    }): RenderedEmail;
+    /**
+     * Send an email notification.
+     *
+     * Performs recipient resolution, template selection, and template
+     * rendering, then stores the composed email. Returns a failure result
+     * if the recipient is not a valid email address.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification payload to deliver via email.
+     * @returns A result indicating delivery success or failure.
+     */
+    send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+    /**
+     * Get all emails sent through this adapter. Useful for testing.
+     */
+    getSentEmails(): SentEmail[];
+    /**
+     * Get emails sent to a specific recipient. Useful for testing.
+     */
+    getSentEmailsTo(recipient: string): SentEmail[];
+    /**
+     * Get the count of sent emails.
+     */
+    getSentEmailCount(): number;
+    /**
+     * Clear all sent emails. Useful for testing.
+     */
+    clearSentEmails(): void;
+}

+/**
+ * Base interface for all notification delivery adapters.
+ *
+ * CONSTRAINT: All notification adapters MUST implement this interface.
+ * This is the extension point for adding new notification channels
+ * (email, SMS, webhook, push notifications, etc.).
+ *
+ * The NotificationService routes payloads to the appropriate adapter
+ * based on the payload's `type` field. Each adapter handles exactly
+ * one NotificationType.
+ *
+ * ## Implementing a new adapter
+ *
+ * 1. Create a new file in src/adapters/ (e.g., sms.adapter.ts)
+ * 2. Implement the NotificationAdapter interface
+ * 3. Register the adapter with NotificationService via `registerAdapter()`
+ *
+ * Example:
+ * ```typescript
+ * export class SmsAdapter implements NotificationAdapter {
+ *   readonly type = 'sms';
+ *   async send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult> {
+ *     // Send SMS via external service...
+ *     return { success: true, messageId: '...', timestamp: new Date() };
+ *   }
+ * }
+ * ```
+ *
+ * ## Adapter lifecycle
+ *
+ * - Adapters are stateless: they receive a payload and return a result.
+ * - Adapters MUST NOT throw exceptions. Return `{ success: false, error: '...' }` instead.
+ * - Adapters MUST NOT subscribe to EventBus directly. Only NotificationService
+ *   subscribes to events; adapters handle delivery only.
+ * - Adapters MAY use Logger for internal diagnostics.
+ */
+import type { NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationType } from '../models/notification.js';
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+    /**
+     * The notification channel this adapter handles.
+     * Must match one of the NotificationType values.
+     * Each type can only have one registered adapter.
+     */
+    readonly type: NotificationType;
+    /**
+     * Deliver a notification through this adapter's channel.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification content to deliver.
+     * @returns A result indicating success or failure. Never throws.
+     */
+    send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

+/**
+ * Event bus for decoupled inter-module communication.
+ *
+ * Architecture decision: This application uses an event-driven pattern
+ * for communication between services. Instead of direct function calls
+ * between modules, services emit events that other services can listen to.
+ *
+ * This promotes loose coupling and makes it easy to add new behaviors
+ * (e.g., new notification channels) without modifying existing services.
+ */
+import type { AppEvent } from './event-types.js';
+export type EventHandler<T extends AppEvent = AppEvent> = (event: T) => void | Promise<void>;
+export declare class EventBus {
+    private handlers;
+    private eventLog;
+    /**
+     * Subscribe to events of a given type.
+     */
+    on<T extends AppEvent>(eventType: T['type'], handler: EventHandler<T>): () => void;
+    /**
+     * Emit an event to all registered handlers.
+     */
+    emit<T extends AppEvent>(event: T): Promise<void>;
+    /**
+     * Get all emitted events. Useful for testing and debugging.
+     */
+    getEventLog(): AppEvent[];
+    /**
+     * Clear all handlers and event log.
+     */
+    clear(): void;
+}

+/**
+ * Notification model types and interfaces.
+ *
+ * These types define the notification domain model used throughout the
+ * notification system. All notification adapters, services, and event
+ * handlers operate on these shared types.
+ *
+ * Architecture: The notification system uses an event-driven pattern.
+ * Services emit domain events (e.g., 'order:created') via EventBus.
+ * The NotificationService subscribes to these events, constructs
+ * NotificationPayload objects, and dispatches them through registered
+ * NotificationAdapter implementations (email, SMS, webhook, etc.).
+ */
+/**
+ * Supported notification delivery channels.
+ *
+ * Each channel corresponds to a NotificationAdapter implementation.
+ * New channels require:
+ * 1. Adding a value here
+ * 2. Implementing a NotificationAdapter for it
+ * 3. Registering the adapter with NotificationService
+ */
+export type NotificationType = 'email' | 'sms' | 'in_app' | 'webhook';
+/**
+ * Lifecycle status of a notification.
+ */
+export type NotificationStatus = 'pending' | 'sent' | 'failed';
+/**
+ * Priority levels for notifications.
+ * Adapters may use this to determine delivery urgency.
+ */
+export type NotificationPriority = 'low' | 'normal' | 'high' | 'urgent';
+/**
+ * The payload sent to a notification adapter for delivery.
+ *
+ * This is the core data structure that flows through the notification
+ * pipeline. Event handlers create payloads; adapters consume them.
+ */
+export interface NotificationPayload {
+    /** The delivery channel for this notification. */
+    type: NotificationType;
+    /** The recipient identifier (email address, phone number, user ID, URL, etc.). */
+    recipient: string;
+    /** Short summary / subject line. */
+    subject: string;
+    /** Full notification body content. */
+    body: string;
+    /** Priority level. Defaults to 'normal' if not specified. */
+    priority?: NotificationPriority;
+    /** Optional structured metadata for adapter-specific features. */
+    metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
+/**
+ * Result returned by a NotificationAdapter after attempting delivery.
+ */
+export interface NotificationResult {
+    /** Whether the notification was delivered successfully. */
+    success: boolean;
+    /** The adapter-assigned message ID, if any. */
+    messageId?: string;
+    /** Human-readable error message on failure. */
+    error?: string;
+    /** ISO timestamp of when the delivery was attempted. */
+    timestamp: Date;
+}
+/**
+ * A persisted notification record combining payload, result, and identity.
+ *
+ * This is what gets stored in the notification log and returned by
+ * NotificationService query methods.
+ */
+export interface Notification {
+    /** Unique notification ID (e.g., 'notif-0001'). */
+    id: string;
+    /** The delivery channel used. */
+    type: NotificationType;
+    /** The recipient identifier. */
+    recipient: string;
+    /** Short summary / subject line. */
+    subject: string;
+    /** Full notification body content. */
+    body: string;
+    /** Current delivery status. */
+    status: NotificationStatus;
+    /** Priority level. */
+    priority: NotificationPriority;
+    /** When the notification was created. */
+    createdAt: Date;
+    /** When the notification was last updated (e.g., delivery attempt). */
+    updatedAt: Date;
+    /** Adapter-assigned message ID, if delivery succeeded. */
+    messageId?: string;
+    /** Error message, if delivery failed. */
+    error?: string;
+}

+/**
+ * Notification service — orchestrates notification delivery in response to
+ * domain events received via EventBus.
+ *
+ * ## Architecture
+ *
+ * This service is the single entry point for all notifications in the system.
+ * It follows a pure event-driven pattern:
+ *
+ * 1. Domain services (UserService, OrderService) emit events via EventBus.
+ * 2. NotificationService subscribes to those events in its constructor.
+ * 3. Event handlers construct NotificationPayload objects.
+ * 4. Payloads are dispatched to the appropriate NotificationAdapter based on
+ *    the payload's `type` field.
+ * 5. Results are logged and the notification is stored in memory.
+ *
+ * ## Constraints
+ *
+ * - ALL notifications MUST flow through EventBus. No direct service calls.
+ * - ALL delivery adapters MUST implement the NotificationAdapter interface.
+ * - Adapters are registered via `registerAdapter()` and looked up by type.
+ * - If no adapter is registered for a payload type, the notification is stored
+ *   with status 'sent' (in-memory only, no external delivery).
+ * - Event handlers MUST NOT throw. Delivery failures are captured in the
+ *   NotificationResult and stored as failed notifications.
+ *
+ * ## Adding new notification triggers
+ *
+ * To send notifications for a new event:
+ * 1. Define the event type in src/events/event-types.ts
+ * 2. Have the originating service emit the event via EventBus
+ * 3. Add a handler in `registerEventHandlers()` below
+ * 4. The handler constructs a NotificationPayload and calls `dispatchNotification()`
+ *
+ * Depends on:
+ * - EventBus (event system) for receiving domain events
+ * - NotificationAdapter implementations for delivery
+ */
+import type { EventBus } from '../events/event-bus.js';
+import type { NotificationAdapter } from '../adapters/notification.adapter.js';
+import type { Notification, NotificationPayload, NotificationType } from '../models/notification.js';
+/**
+ * Reset the notification ID counter. For testing only.
+ */
+export declare function resetNotificationIdCounter(): void;
+export declare class NotificationService {
+    private eventBus;
+    private logger;
+    private notifications;
+    private adapters;
+    constructor(eventBus: EventBus);
+    /**
+     * Register a notification adapter for a specific delivery channel.
+     *
+     * Each NotificationType can have exactly one adapter. Registering a
+     * second adapter for the same type replaces the previous one.
+     *
+     * @param adapter - The adapter to register.
+     */
+    registerAdapter(adapter: NotificationAdapter): void;
+    /**
+     * Unregister the adapter for a specific notification type.
+     *
+     * @param type - The notification channel to unregister.
+     * @returns true if an adapter was removed, false if none was registered.
+     */
+    unregisterAdapter(type: NotificationType): boolean;
+    /**
+     * Check whether an adapter is registered for the given type.
+     */
+    hasAdapter(type: NotificationType): boolean;
+    /**
+     * Get all stored notifications.
+     */
+    getNotifications(): Notification[];
+    /**
+     * Get notifications for a specific recipient.
+     */
+    getNotificationsForRecipient(recipient: string): Notification[];
+    /**
+     * Get the count of stored notifications.
+     */
+    getNotificationCount(): number;
+    /**
+     * Clear all stored notifications. Useful for testing.
+     */
+    clearNotifications(): void;
+    /**
+     * Subscribe to domain events from the EventBus.
+     *
+     * All notification triggers are registered here. To add a new trigger,
+     * subscribe to the relevant event type and construct a NotificationPayload.
+     */
+    private registerEventHandlers;
+    /**
+     * Handle order created event — send order confirmation email.
+     */
+    private handleOrderCreated;
+    /**
+     * Handle order status change — notify user of update.
+     */
+    private handleOrderStatusChanged;
+    /**
+     * Dispatch a notification through the appropriate adapter.
+     *
+     * This is the central dispatch method. It:
+     * 1. Creates a Notification record with 'pending' status
+     * 2. Looks up the adapter for the payload's type
+     * 3. Calls the adapter's send() method
+     * 4. Updates the notification status based on the result
+     *
+     * If no adapter is registered for the type, the notification is
+     * stored with 'sent' status (in-memory only, no external delivery).
+     * This preserves backward compatibility and allows the system to
+     * function without any adapters registered.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification content to deliver.
+     * @returns The stored Notification record.
+     */
+    dispatchNotification(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<Notification>;
+}

+/**
+ * Callback registry for direct service-to-service notification.
+ *
+ * Alternative approach to EventBus: register callbacks directly on services
+ * for lightweight, synchronous notification without an intermediary.
+ *
+ * Trade-offs vs EventBus:
+ * - Simpler (no event types, no serialization)
+ * - Synchronous by default (predictable ordering)
+ * - Tighter coupling (caller knows about callbacks)
+ * - No event log / replay capability
+ */
+export declare class CallbackRegistry<T = unknown> {
+    private callbacks;
+    /**
+     * Register a callback to be notified when events occur.
+     */
+    register(cb: (data: T) => void): () => void;
+    /**
+     * Notify all registered callbacks with the given data.
+     */
+    notify(data: T): void;
+    /**
+     * Get the number of registered callbacks.
+     */
+    get size(): number;
+    /**
+     * Clear all registered callbacks.
+     */
+    clear(): void;
+}
+import type { OrderStatusChangedEvent } from '../events/event-types.js';
+/**
+ * Registry for order status change callbacks.
+ * Services register here to be notified of status changes
+ * via the direct callback pattern (as opposed to EventBus).
+ */
+export declare const statusChangeCallbacks: CallbackRegistry<OrderStatusChangedEvent>;

+/**
+ * Simple in-memory database for the application.
+ *
+ * Provides basic CRUD operations with collection-based storage.
+ * This is the utility layer — the deepest dependency in the architecture.
+ *
+ * Architecture note: All data access goes through Repository classes,
+ * which depend on this Database class. Direct database access from
+ * services is an anti-pattern in this codebase.
+ */
+export interface DatabaseRecord {
+    id: string;
+    [key: string]: unknown;
+}
+export declare class Database {
+    private collections;
+    /**
+     * Get or create a collection.
+     */
+    private getCollection;
+    /**
+     * Insert a record into a collection.
+     */
+    insert(collection: string, record: DatabaseRecord): DatabaseRecord;
+    /**
+     * Find a record by ID.
+     */
+    findById(collection: string, id: string): DatabaseRecord | undefined;
+    /**
+     * Find all records in a collection, optionally filtered by a predicate.
+     */
+    findAll(collection: string, predicate?: (record: DatabaseRecord) => boolean): DatabaseRecord[];
+    /**
+     * Update a record by ID. Merges the update into the existing record.
+     */
+    update(collection: string, id: string, update: Partial<DatabaseRecord>): DatabaseRecord;
+    /**
+     * Delete a record by ID.
+     */
+    delete(collection: string, id: string): boolean;
+    /**
+     * Count records in a collection.
+     */
+    count(collection: string): number;
+    /**
+     * Clear all data from all collections.
+     */
+    clear(): void;
+}
+export declare function getDatabase(): Database;
+export declare function resetDatabase(): void;

+/**
+ * Pagination utilities for list endpoints.
+ */
+export interface PaginationResult<T> {
+    /** Items on the current page */
+    items: T[];
+    /** Current page number (1-indexed) */
+    page: number;
+    /** Number of items per page */
+    pageSize: number;
+    /** Total number of items across all pages */
+    totalItems: number;
+    /** Total number of pages */
+    totalPages: number;
+    /** Whether there is a next page */
+    hasNext: boolean;
+    /** Whether there is a previous page */
+    hasPrevious: boolean;
+}
+/**
+ * Paginate an array of items.
+ *
+ * @param items - The full array of items to paginate
+ * @param page - The page number to retrieve (1-indexed)
+ * @param pageSize - Number of items per page
+ * @returns Paginated result with metadata
+ */
+export declare function paginate<T>(items: T[], page: number, pageSize: number): PaginationResult<T>;
